import xlrd

# Table 3.3 - find key columns
wb = xlrd.open_workbook\('data/raw/2020/20in33ar.xls'\)
sh = wb.sheet_by_index\(0\)

# Search for total income tax and total credits
for c in range\(100, sh.ncols\):
    for r in range\(2, 8\):
        v = str\(sh.cell_value\(r, c\)\)
        if v.strip\(\):
            print\(f'  col {c}, row {r}: {repr\(v\)[:80]}  -> r9={sh.cell_value\(9, c\)}'\)

print\(\)
# Row 9 = All returns, get the key values we need
print\('=== Table 3.3 row 9 \(All returns\) key values ==='\)
print\(f'  col 1 \(return count\): {sh.cell_value\(9, 1\)}'\)
# Find "total income tax" amount column
for c in [118, 119, 120, 121, 122, 123, 124, 125, 126, 127, 128, 129, 130]:
    r2 = sh.cell_value\(2, c\)
    r3 = sh.cell_value\(3, c\)
    r6 = sh.cell_value\(6, c\)
    v = sh.cell_value\(9, c\)
    print\(f'  col {c}: h2={repr\(r2\)[:40]} h3={repr\(r3\)[:40]} h6={repr\(r6\)[:40]} val={v}'\)

# Verify: Table 1.1 total income tax was at col 16, r9 = 1710685785
# Table 3.3 should have similar total
print\(\)

# Table 1.2 structure - filing status breakdown
print\('=== TABLE 1.2 ==='\)
wb2 = xlrd.open_workbook\('data/raw/2020/20in12ms.xls'\)
sh2 = wb2.sheet_by_index\(0\)
print\(f'{sh2.nrows}r x {sh2.ncols}c'\)
for r in range\(10\):
    labeled = [\(c, sh2.cell_value\(r, c\)\) for c in range\(min\(8, sh2.ncols\)\) if sh2.cell_value\(r, c\) != '']
    print\(f'  row {r}: {labeled}'\)
for r in [10, 11, 12]:
    vals = [sh2.cell_value\(r, c\) for c in range\(min\(8, sh2.ncols\)\)]
    print\(f'  row {r}: {vals}'\)
# find what row has filing status labels
for r in range\(8, min\(60, sh2.nrows\)\):
    v0 = str\(sh2.cell_value\(r, 0\)\).strip\(\)
    if 'married' in v0.lower\(\) or 'single' in v0.lower\(\) or 'head' in v0.lower\(\) or 'all returns' in v0.lower\(\):
        print\(f'  row {r}: {repr\(v0\)[:70]}'\)
PYEOF)

import xlrd

# Table 1.2 - find filing status sections and column structure
wb = xlrd.open_workbook\('data/raw/2020/20in12ms.xls'\)
sh = wb.sheet_by_index\(0\)

# Check for filing status in headers \(row 2\)
print\('=== Table 1.2 header row 2 ==='\)
for c in range\(sh.ncols\):
    v = sh.cell_value\(2, c\)
    if v:
        print\(f'  col {c}: {repr\(v\)[:60]}'\)

# Check rows 3-4 for column structure across all filing statuses
print\('=== Table 1.2 header row 3 ==='\)
for c in range\(sh.ncols\):
    v = sh.cell_value\(3, c\)
    if v:
        print\(f'  col {c}: {repr\(v\)[:60]}'\)

# What are the filing status column groups?
# Row 2 has the filing status headers

# Table 3.2 - understand column for total AGI and total income tax
print\(\)
print\('=== TABLE 3.2 key columns ==='\)
wb = xlrd.open_workbook\('data/raw/2020/20in32tt.xls'\)
sh = wb.sheet_by_index\(0\)
# Only care about first 3 data columns \(Total section\)
# col 1 = return count, col 2 = AGI, col 3 = total income tax
for r in [9, 10, 11]:
    print\(f'  row {r}: col0={repr\(sh.cell_value\(r, 0\)\)[:30]}, count={sh.cell_value\(r, 1\)}, agi={sh.cell_value\(r, 2\)}, tax={sh.cell_value\(r, 3\)}'\)

# Table 3.2 uses different AGI bins - check them
print\('=== Table 3.2 AGI bin labels ==='\)
for r in range\(9, 27\):
    v0 = sh.cell_value\(r, 0\)
    if v0:
        print\(f'  row {r}: {repr\(v0\)[:60]}'\)

# Check 2021 and 2022 have same structure
print\(\)
print\('=== YEAR CONSISTENCY CHECK ==='\)
for year in [2021, 2022]:
    yy = str\(year\)[2:]
    wb = xlrd.open_workbook\(f'data/raw/{year}/{yy}in11si.xls'\)
    sh = wb.sheet_by_index\(0\)
    # Check row 9 col 0 is 'All returns'
    print\(f'  {year} Table 1.1: r9c0={repr\(sh.cell_value\(9, 0\)\)}, r7 nums={sh.cell_value\(7, 1\)}, rows={sh.nrows}, cols={sh.ncols}'\)
    wb = xlrd.open_workbook\(f'data/raw/{year}/{yy}in34tr.xls'\)
    sh = wb.sheet_by_index\(0\)
    print\(f'  {year} Table 3.4: r8c0={repr\(sh.cell_value\(8, 0\)\)}, r9c0={repr\(sh.cell_value\(9, 0\)\)}, rows={sh.nrows}, cols={sh.ncols}'\)
PYEOF)
